notes: Evolved to Windows 2000 Server, Windows Server 2003/2008/2012/2019/2022. Became dominant enterprise OS.
HP/Microsoft joint strategy announced March 1997. Included TCO reduction, joint application solutions, heterogeneous interoperability. Dissolved when HP acquired Compaq in 2002.
HP planned to port OpenView IT/Operations to NT by year-end 1997 as part of SEC. Evolved into HP Software; renamed to Micro Focus Operations Bridge after 2017 Micro Focus acquisition.
HP endorsed Exchange as strategic NT messaging; HP OpenMail to provide migration services. Exchange remains a major Microsoft product.
HP co-developed Merced with Intel; expected early access advantage. Itanium shipped 2001; discontinued 2021 after failing to displace x86-64.
HP endorsed Wolfpack as strategic NT HA clustering. Wolfpack became Microsoft Cluster Service (MSCS) in Windows 2000; now Windows Server Failover Clustering.
Tandem's hardware clustering architecture for NT high-end. Post-Compaq acquisition, evolved into HPE NonStop systems.
HP VPA allowed bundled discounts across printers, networking, servers. Aberdeen cited as major competitive advantage over Compaq.
HP's internal PC management environment saving $200M/year on 100,000+ employees. Being made externally available as part of SEC.
Low-cost NetPC at $1,000 entry-level announced as part of SEC. Network PC concept faded as thin clients and standard PCs dominated.
